title: Enterprise Health Overview Dashboard
description: As an internal ops/engineering user, I want a top-level dashboard that lists all enterprises onboarded in GIP along with a real-time health snapshot so I can immediately identify which enterprise has sync or delivery issues.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to see all enterprises in a single view with their GIP sync health so I can quickly spot broken integrations without drilling into each enterprise individually.
acceptanceCriteria: List all active enterprises from GIP `enterprises` collection (where deletedOn is null and liveStatus = 1)
Each row shows: Enterprise name (tradeName), baCode, ssoEnterpriseId, linked Zwing vendor ID (meta.zwingVId)

Each row shows aggregated metrics for a configurable default time window (last 24h): total Zwing invoices generated, total GIP ingress events captured, sync gap count (Zwing invoices with no matching GIP job), total GIP jobs, success count, failed count, failure rate %
Overall health status badge per enterprise: GREEN (failure rate < 2%), YELLOW (2–10%), RED (> 10% or any debezium gap detected)
Table is paginated (25 per page), sortable by enterprise name, failure rate, sync gap
Global date-range filter (presets: last 1h, 6h, 24h, 7d, 30d; custom range) applied to all metrics
Search/filter by enterprise name or baCode
Clicking an enterprise row navigates to the Enterprise Detail view (STORY-002)

gipJobsAggregation.metrics: count by status (success, failed, pending, processing)

technicalDetails.invoiceCountStrategy: Query each enterprise's tenant DB (db_name from vendor table) for invoice count in date range. Use connection pooling against the shared MySQL instance; route using db_name from vendor table.
notes: Zwing tenant DBs are on the same MySQL instance; use db_name from vendor table to switch schema per enterprise
To detect sync gap: COUNT(Zwing invoices) - COUNT(distinct dipJobs.refDocNo WHERE ssoEnterpriseId matches) in same time window
Cache enterprise list; refresh every 5 minutes

title: Enterprise Detail View with Connector & App Health
description: As an ops engineer, I want a detailed view for a single enterprise showing all its GIP connectors, the apps connected, and the health of each connector so I can pinpoint which integration path is broken.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to drill into a specific enterprise and see all connector pairs (e.g., Zwing → ERP, Zwing → CRM) with their event-level health so I can diagnose which connector or event type is failing.
acceptanceCriteria: Show enterprise header: tradeName, baCode, ssoEnterpriseId, Zwing vendor ID, Zwing db_name
List all connectors for this enterprise from GIP `connectors` collection, showing: connector name, outbound app name, inbound app name, isEnabled status, deletedOn (deleted badge if soft-deleted)
For each connector, show aggregated job metrics in the selected date range: total jobs, success, failed, pending, failure rate %
For each connector, show list of mapped event types (from connectorEventMappings: outboundEvent.name + inboundEvent.name)
Connector status badge: ACTIVE (isEnabled true, deletedOn null), DISABLED (isEnabled false), DELETED (deletedOn not null)
Date range filter applies to job metrics
Clicking a connector row expands to show per-event-type job breakdown (inline)
Navigation breadcrumb: Dashboard > Enterprise Name

apps.purpose: Resolve outboundAppId and inboundAppId to app names

events.purpose: Resolve outboundEventId and inboundEventId to event names and eventCodes

title: Debezium Sync Gap Detection (Zwing → GIP Reconciliation)
description: As an ops engineer, I want to compare the number of invoices generated in Zwing against the number of events captured in GIP via Debezium so I can identify missed events caused by connector breaks or Kafka lag.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to see for each enterprise how many Zwing invoices were NOT captured as GIP ingress events in a given time window so I can detect and report Debezium failures early.
acceptanceCriteria: For a selected enterprise and date range, show side-by-side counts: 'Zwing Invoice Count' (from tenant DB) vs 'GIP Events Received' (distinct dipJobs.refDocNo)
Show 'Sync Gap' = Zwing Invoice Count - GIP Events Received, highlighted RED if gap > 0
List the specific invoice_ids (refDocNo) present in Zwing but absent in GIP dipJobs — these are the missed events
Missed events table shows: invoice_id, store_id, transaction_type, transaction_sub_type, invoice date, Zwing status
Ability to filter missed events by transaction_type (sales/return), transaction_sub_type, store_id
Show event breakdown by Kafka topic / eventCode (invoices, gv_invoices, etc.) to identify which topic is lagging
Refresh button to re-run reconciliation query on demand
Export missed events list as CSV

zwingQuery.query: SELECT invoice_id, store_id, transaction_type, transaction_sub_type, status, created_at FROM invoices WHERE created_at BETWEEN :from AND :to AND deleted_at IS NULL
zwingQuery.note: Filter out voided/deleted invoices as appropriate. status IN ('Success', 'Processing') for valid invoices

gipQuery.note: refDocNo in dipJobs corresponds to invoice_id in Zwing invoices table (set via events.refDocPath: 'after.invoice_id')

technicalDetails.reconciliationLogic: SET(Zwing invoice_ids) MINUS SET(GIP dipJobs.refDocNo) = missed events. Cross-reference ingressEventLogId presence to confirm Debezium received vs GIP consumed.
technicalDetails.performanceNote: Zwing queries run against production MySQL — use read replicas. Paginate missed events list. For large enterprises, run reconciliation as background job and cache results with TTL.

title: GIP Job Status Dashboard per Enterprise
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see the real-time status of GIP jobs (pending, processing, success, failed) for each enterprise across all connectors and event types so I can monitor end-to-end delivery health.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want a job status summary for each enterprise so I can know whether events received from Zwing are being successfully delivered to downstream apps like ERP or CRM.
acceptanceCriteria: Show job status summary for selected enterprise and date range: total, pending, processing, success, failed counts with % breakdown
Breakdown by connector (Zwing→ERP, Zwing→CRM, etc.): each connector row shows success/failed counts
Breakdown by event type (eventCode): table showing event name, eventCode, volume, success rate, avg processing time
Time-series chart: job volume and failure rate over the selected time window (hourly buckets)
Top failed events list ordered by failure count
Filter by: connector, inbound app, event type, status
Auto-refresh every 60 seconds when date range includes 'now'
Failure rate RED threshold configurable (default 5%)

aggregation: Match by ssoEnterpriseId and transactionDate range
Lookup connectors to get connector name and app names
Lookup connectorEventMappings → events to get eventCode and event name

technicalDetails.timeSeries: Bucket by transactionDate using MongoDB $dateTrunc (1-hour buckets), group by status, return counts per bucket

title: Failed Jobs List with Error Details
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see a list of all failed GIP jobs with error messages, retry counts, and affected transactions so I can investigate and resolve delivery failures.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to view all failed jobs with full context (enterprise, connector, event type, invoice ID, error message, retry count) so I can diagnose root causes and take corrective action.
acceptanceCriteria: List all failed jobs (status = 'failed') for the selected filters, paginated (50 per page)

Each row shows: enterprise name, connector name, outbound app → inbound app, eventCode, refDocNo (invoice_id), transactionDate, retryCount, isRetryable, latest error message (from error field or timestamps array last entry)
Sort by: transactionDate (newest first, default), retryCount (highest first), enterprise name
Filters: enterprise (multi-select), connector, inbound app, event type, date range, retryCount range
Clicking a row opens Job Detail panel (see STORY-006)
Bulk selection: select multiple jobs and trigger bulk retry (only for isRetryable=true jobs)
Export filtered failed jobs to CSV
Show 'isRetryable' badge — non-retryable jobs shown with explanation tooltip

technicalDetails.errorExtraction: Use dipJobs.error field. If empty, use last entry in dipJobs.timestamps array where status='failed' for error context.

title: Job Detail & Task-Level Drill Down
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see the full details of a single GIP job including all its tasks (filter, transform, http dispatch, etc.) with individual statuses and errors so I can understand exactly where in the pipeline a failure occurred.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to open a specific job and see its execution timeline, each task's status and error, so I can identify the precise step (e.g., HTTP dispatch to ERP) that failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Job header shows: jobId, enterprise, connector, eventCode, refDocNo (invoice_id), transactionDate, overall status, retryCount, isRetryable
Status timeline: visual stepper showing all timestamps entries (pending → processing → success/failed) with timestamps
Task list from dipJobTasks collection: task name, type (filter/http/transform), status, retryCount, error message, createdAt, updatedAt
For HTTP tasks: show httpMetadataPath reference with note to fetch metadata from object storage for request/response details
For failed tasks: show full error message prominently
Show continueOnFailure flag per task
Parent-child task relationships visualized (parentId linkage)
Retry button for the whole job if isRetryable=true
Navigation: back to failed jobs list, breadcrumb with enterprise and connector

title: Invoice / Transaction Trace (End-to-End Lookup)
description: As an ops engineer or support agent, I want to search for a specific invoice ID and see its complete journey from Zwing → Debezium → GIP → downstream app so I can answer 'did this invoice reach the ERP?' in seconds.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to enter an invoice_id and see the full sync pipeline status for that invoice — from Zwing invoice record through Debezium capture and all GIP jobs — so I can quickly confirm or diagnose delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input accepts invoice_id (Zwing format, e.g. CLPA010125263285) or partial match
Step 1 - Zwing Record: Show invoice details from Zwing tenant DB: invoice_id, store_id, transaction_type, transaction_sub_type, status (Success/Failed/Void/Processing), created_at, total, v_id
Step 2 - GIP Ingress: Show whether a dipJob exists with matching refDocNo. If yes: show ingressEventLogId, transactionDate, eventCode. If no: show 'NOT CAPTURED — possible Debezium miss'
Step 3 - GIP Jobs: List all dipJobs with this refDocNo (could be multiple connectors): connector name, outbound→inbound app, status, retryCount
Step 4 - Task Execution: For each job, show task summary (total tasks, success, failed)
Overall pipeline status: SYNCED (all jobs success), PARTIAL (some jobs failed), NOT SYNCED (no GIP job found)
Ability to search across all enterprises (requires knowing enterprise context or global search with ssoEnterpriseId lookup)
Support searching by enterprise scope to narrow Zwing DB query

searchFlow: 2. Query GIP dipJobs: db.dipJobs.find({ refDocNo: <invoice_id> }) — no enterprise scope needed in GIP
3. From dipJobs, get ssoEnterpriseId → lookup enterprise → get meta.zwingVId → lookup vendor.db_name
4. Query Zwing tenant DB (db_name): SELECT * FROM invoices WHERE invoice_id = :invoiceId
5. Fetch all dipJobTasks for each matched jobId
technicalDetails.multiConnectorNote: One invoice can generate multiple jobs if enterprise has multiple connectors (Zwing→ERP and Zwing→CRM). Show all.
apiEndpoints: GET /api/dashboard/trace?invoiceId=&ssoEnterpriseId= (ssoEnterpriseId optional if GIP has the refDocNo)

title: Per-App Delivery Health (Inbound App Perspective)
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see delivery health from the perspective of each downstream app (ERP, CRM, MSG91, SSO) across all enterprises so I can identify if a specific app is the common point of failure.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to see how many jobs targeted at the ERP are failing across all enterprises so I can determine if the issue is the app itself versus a specific enterprise's integration.
acceptanceCriteria: List all apps from GIP `apps` collection (name, refId, isEnabled)
For each app (as inbound app), show: total jobs targeted in date range, success, failed, pending, failure rate %
Cross-enterprise breakdown: for each app, show per-enterprise job health
Show connector list per app across all enterprises (connector name, enterprise name, isEnabled)
Filter by: date range, app, enterprise
Highlight apps with failure rate > threshold in RED
Sort by failure rate descending

title: Event Type Health Matrix (Across Enterprises & Apps)
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see a health matrix of all event types (invoice.share, return.invoice, etc.) across all enterprises and connectors so I can spot if a specific event type is systematically failing.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to know if the 'zwing.invoice.share.v1' event is failing only for one enterprise or across all of them, so I can distinguish a data issue from a systemic platform bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix view: rows = event types (eventCode), columns = enterprises (or apps), cells = failure rate with color coding (GREEN/YELLOW/RED)
Alternatively, list view: event name, eventCode, total jobs, success, failed, failure rate, affected enterprises count
Clicking a cell or event row shows filtered failed jobs list for that event + enterprise combination
Filter by: date range, app (filter events by appId), direction (inbound/outbound)
Event types sourced from `events` collection, grouped by eventCode and appId
Show Kafka topic name per event (listenerConfig.kafkaTopicName) for Debezium correlation

dipJobs.aggregation: Group by connectorAppEventId, ssoEnterpriseId, status. Lookup connectorEventMappings → events to get eventCode.

title: Retry Management for Failed Jobs
description: As an ops engineer, I want to manually trigger retries for failed GIP jobs (individually or in bulk) so I can recover missed deliveries without engineering intervention.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to select one or more failed jobs and click 'Retry' so that GIP reprocesses them and delivers the events to the downstream app.
acceptanceCriteria: Single job retry: available on Job Detail view (STORY-006) with confirmation dialog showing job summary
Bulk retry: available on Failed Jobs List (STORY-005) after selecting multiple jobs; only retryable jobs (isRetryable=true) can be selected for retry
Retry by filter: allow retry of all failed jobs matching current filter (enterprise + connector + event type + date range) with count confirmation before execution
On retry: system resets job status to 'pending' and enqueues for reprocessing via GIP's retry mechanism
Show retry progress: toast notification with count of jobs submitted for retry
After retry, jobs list refreshes and shows updated statuses
Retry audit log: show who triggered retry, when, how many jobs (stored in a meta field or separate audit collection)
Non-retryable jobs (isRetryable=false) show disabled retry button with tooltip explaining why

technicalDetails.retryMechanism: Trigger GIP's existing retry API or directly update dipJobs status to 'pending' and reset retryCount if within allowed max retries. Confirm with GIP team which endpoint handles retry re-enqueue.
apiEndpoints: POST /api/dashboard/jobs/retry-by-filter (body: { ssoEnterpriseId, connectorId, from, to, eventId })
validation: Only retry jobs with status='failed' and isRetryable=true
Enforce max batch size of 500 jobs per retry operation
Rate limit retry API to prevent abuse

title: Global Cross-Enterprise Summary (Operations Command Center)
description: As an ops manager, I want a single command-center view showing key health metrics across ALL enterprises simultaneously — total events today, overall sync rate, total failures — so I can monitor platform health at a glance.
userStory: As an ops manager, I want a high-level summary page showing platform-wide metrics so I can detect widespread issues (like a global Debezium outage) immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Top KPI cards: Total Enterprises Active, Total Zwing Invoices Today, Total GIP Jobs Today, Overall Success Rate %, Total Failed Jobs Today, Sync Gap (invoices without GIP event)
Bar chart: top 10 enterprises by job volume in selected date range
Bar chart: top 10 enterprises by failure count
Line chart: platform-wide job volume and failure rate over time (hourly)
App health summary row: for each app (ERP, CRM, MSG91, SSO), show failure rate pill
Alert banner: if any enterprise has sync gap > 10 or failure rate > 20%, show prominent alert
Date range filter applies to all widgets
Auto-refresh every 2 minutes

dataSources.dipJobs: Aggregate across all ssoEnterpriseIds, group by status and transactionDate
dataSources.zwing: Sum of invoice counts across all tenant DBs — expensive query; run as scheduled job (every 15 min) and cache result

technicalDetails.cachingStrategy: Cache aggregated metrics in Redis or MongoDB summary collection. Background job computes and stores metrics every 5 minutes. Dashboard reads from cache, not live queries.

title: Debezium Connector Status Monitor
description: As an ops engineer, I want to see the status of Debezium connectors per enterprise and Kafka topic so I can know if a connector is broken, lagging, or stopped emitting events.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to see which Debezium connectors are active, which are broken, and how much lag each topic has so I can proactively fix connector failures before invoices are missed.
acceptanceCriteria: List Kafka topics mapped to Zwing events (from events.listenerConfig.kafkaTopicName): e.g., invoices, gv_invoices
Per topic, show: last event timestamp received in GIP (from dipJobs.transactionDate or ingressEventLog), events received in last 1h/24h
Lag indicator: time since last event received per topic per enterprise — flag if > 15 minutes with no events (during business hours)
Show enterprise-topic matrix: which enterprise has which Kafka topics active
Note: actual Debezium connector status (Kafka Connect REST API) if accessible; otherwise infer from event flow gap
Alert: flag enterprises where Zwing invoice activity exists (recent updated_at in invoices table) but no corresponding GIP events in same window

technicalDetails.inferenceApproach: Since direct Kafka Connect API access may not be available, infer Debezium health by: (1) check if any Zwing invoices were created in last N minutes for the enterprise, (2) check if any GIP events (dipJobs) were received for that enterprise+topic in same window. Gap = connector likely broken.
technicalDetails.kafkaTopics: Mapped from events collection: events.listenerConfig.kafkaTopicName. Key topics: 'invoices', 'gv_invoices'
technicalDetails.zwingQuery: SELECT MAX(created_at) as last_invoice_time FROM invoices (per tenant DB) to detect if Zwing is producing data

title: Dashboard Filters, URL State & Saved Views
description: As a dashboard user, I want all filter selections (date range, enterprise, app, event type, status) to be persisted in the URL so I can share links to specific filtered views with teammates.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want to set my preferred filters and bookmark the URL so that every time I open the dashboard I see the same filtered view I care about, and I can share the exact view with teammates.
acceptanceCriteria: All active filters reflected in URL query parameters: from, to, ssoEnterpriseId, connectorId, appId, eventId, status, page
Filter state restored from URL on page load
Global date range selector with presets: Last 1 Hour, Last 6 Hours, Last 24 Hours, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days, Custom Range
Enterprise multi-select filter (typeahead search by name/baCode)
App multi-select filter
Status filter: All, Success, Failed, Pending, Processing
Clear All Filters button resets to defaults
Filter panel collapsible to maximize table area
Active filter chips shown below filter panel for quick review

technicalDetails.implementation: Use URL search params (URLSearchParams API or React Router). Encode multi-select as repeated params or comma-separated.
technicalDetails.frameworkNote: If React: use useSearchParams or react-router. If Next.js: use router.query with shallow routing.

title: Backend API Layer — Core Data Service
description: Build the backend API service that powers the dashboard, connecting to both GIP MongoDB and Zwing MySQL, and exposing REST endpoints for all dashboard stories.
userStory: As a developer, I want a well-structured backend API that efficiently queries GIP MongoDB and Zwing MySQL so that the dashboard frontend has reliable, performant data endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: REST API server (Node.js/Express or equivalent) with structured route organization by domain: /enterprises, /jobs, /apps, /events, /debezium, /trace
MongoDB connection to GIP database with connection pooling (Mongoose or native driver)
MySQL connection pool for Zwing master DB (vendor table) and dynamic per-enterprise tenant DB switching using db_name from vendor table
All list endpoints support pagination (page, limit params, max 200), sorting (sortBy, sortOrder), and date range filtering (from, to as ISO timestamps)
All endpoints return consistent response envelope: { success: bool, data: any, meta: { total, page, limit, from, to } }
Request validation with 400 errors for missing/invalid params
Error handling middleware returning structured errors
Authentication: internal auth (API key or JWT from internal SSO) — all endpoints require authentication
Health check endpoint: GET /health returning DB connectivity status for both MySQL and MongoDB
API documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI) auto-generated or manually written

technicalDetails.recommendedStack: Node.js with Express or Fastify; Mongoose for MongoDB; mysql2 with connection pool for MySQL; Redis for caching
technicalDetails.mysqlMultiTenantStrategy: Maintain a connection pool per db_name, lazily initialized. Vendor table queried first to resolve db_name from ssoEnterpriseId. Cache db_name→enterprise mapping in memory (invalidate every 10 min).
technicalDetails.caching: Redis with TTL-based caching for: enterprise list (5 min), per-enterprise connector list (5 min), aggregated summaries (2 min), reconciliation results (15 min)

title: Frontend Dashboard Application (SPA Shell)
description: Build the frontend single-page application shell for the dashboard with routing, layout, navigation, and authentication.
userStory: As a dashboard user, I want a fast, navigable web application with a consistent layout and sidebar navigation so I can move between different views without losing context.
acceptanceCriteria: React (or Next.js) SPA with TypeScript
Sidebar navigation: Global Summary, Enterprises, Jobs (Failed), Apps, Events, Transaction Trace
Top bar: environment indicator (prod/staging), logged-in user, global date range selector
Authentication: integrate with internal SSO/Okta; redirect to login if unauthenticated
Responsive layout (desktop primary; mobile read-only acceptable)
Loading states, empty states, and error states for all data views
Toast notification system for retry actions and async operations
Dark mode optional but consistent design system (Ant Design, MUI, or Tailwind + shadcn/ui recommended)
Route structure: /, /enterprises, /enterprises/:ssoEnterpriseId, /enterprises/:ssoEnterpriseId/sync-gap, /jobs, /jobs/:jobId, /apps, /events, /trace

technicalDetails.recommendedStack: Next.js 14+ (App Router) with TypeScript; TanStack Query for data fetching and caching; shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS; Recharts or Chart.js for charts; React Table (TanStack Table) for data tables
technicalDetails.stateManagement: TanStack Query for server state; URL params for filter state; minimal client-side state (Zustand if needed)
technicalDetails.buildAndDeploy: Docker container; environment variables for API base URL and auth config

title: Sync Gap Scheduled Reconciliation Job
description: Build a background scheduled job that runs reconciliation between Zwing invoices and GIP events at regular intervals and stores results, so the dashboard can show near-real-time sync gap without expensive on-demand queries.
userStory: As an ops engineer, I want the sync gap to be pre-computed every 15 minutes so the dashboard loads fast and I always have a recent view of missed events without waiting for a slow cross-DB query.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduled job runs every 15 minutes (cron)
For each active enterprise: query Zwing invoice count for last 1 hour and last 24 hours; query GIP dipJobs refDocNo count for same windows; compute and store gap
Results stored in a dedicated MongoDB collection 'syncGapSnapshots': { ssoEnterpriseId, computedAt, window: '1h'|'24h', zwingCount, gipCount, gap, missedInvoiceIds: [] }
Dashboard reads from syncGapSnapshots for fast display; on-demand re-reconcile available but rate-limited
Job reports its own run status (success/fail/lastRunAt) in a 'jobRuns' collection
Alert if gap > 0 for any enterprise for more than 30 minutes consecutively (write to alerts collection)
Job skips enterprises with liveStatus != 1 or no Zwing db_name configured

technicalDetails.scheduler: Node.js cron job (node-cron) or dedicated queue worker (BullMQ). If containerized, run as separate service.
technicalDetails.concurrency: Process enterprises in parallel batches of 5 to avoid overwhelming MySQL
technicalDetails.missedInvoiceIds: For the gap list, store max 1000 invoice_ids per snapshot. For larger gaps, store count only and fetch live on demand.
technicalDetails.mongoCollection: syncGapSnapshots with TTL index on computedAt (expire after 7 days)
